sourceMovie.title: L.A. Confidential
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit L.A. Confidential for the same reason it broke through in 1997: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Curtis Hanson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If L.A. Confidential clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Jackie Brown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If L.A. Confidential clicked for you, Jackie Brown (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after L.A. Confidential?
faq.answer: Try A Walk Among the Tombstones, Jackie Brown, Gone Baby Gone—each shares DNA with L.A. Confidential while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is L.A. Confidential?
faq.answer: L.A. Confidential (1997) is best described as Crime, Mystery, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is L.A. Confidential worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
